title: Echo Chamber Formation
description: Watch how people with similar views cluster together and disconnect from opposing views — until two completely separate information worlds exist.

homophily.label: Homophily
homophily.description: Tendency to prefer similar others (0=random, 1=only connect with identical views)

narration.text: Blue and red represent people with different views — randomly distributed, as in the real world at the start of most social networks.
People subtly move toward those who agree with them. Each small step seems harmless — who doesn't prefer agreeable company?
Clusters are forming. Blues and reds are increasingly separated. Cross-view interactions are becoming rare.
Two distinct communities now exist. Information flows freely within each group but rarely crosses between them.
Each community now has different facts, different threats, different heroes. They're not just disagreeing — they're living in different information realities.
This happened with no central control, no algorithm, no conspiracy. Just the individual preference to spend time with people who agree. Homophily at scale.
